description_long: This object is a small windmill mounted atop a tall, slender wooden post. The windmill features six evenly spaced blue blades radiating from a central hub, designed to catch the wind efficiently. At the rear, a triangular tail vane, likely made of metal, helps orient the blades toward the wind. The post appears to be made of treated wood, providing stability and height for optimal wind exposure. The overall construction is simple and utilitarian, suitable for small-scale wind energy generation or decorative purposes in urban parks or gardens. The visible components suggest durability and weather resistance for outdoor use.
description: A small blue-bladed windmill on a tall wooden post with a rear metal tail vane, designed for outdoor use.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all six blue blades radiating from the central hub atop the wooden post.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blades and the full triangular tail vane extending from the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the central hub, all six blades, and the top of the wooden post.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tail vane and the edge-on view of the blades.
